concerns.text: Tasks T013-T015 implement a dataset size of N=200 examples. This silently weakens FR-002 and US-1 which mandate 'at least 500 examples'. The Plan justifies this reduction for feasibility, but the tasks do not reflect a formal spec amendment or explicit 'staged' acceptance criteria. Implementing 200 when the spec demands 500 is a direct constraint violation unless the spec is updated to reflect the staged MVP.

Task T013 specifies using 'Phi-3-mini' as the Oracle model. FR-002 and US-1 explicitly mandate 'Llama-3-8B' (or similar). While the Plan notes this override for feasibility, the task description does not cite the Plan override or explicitly state that this is a deviation from the spec's functional requirement. This constitutes a silent relaxation of the Oracle Ground-Truth constraint (Constitution Principle VII).

Task T018 specifies using 'sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2' for semantic similarity. FR-003 explicitly mandates 'Llama-3-8B last-layer embedding space'. The Plan overrides this to avoid circularity, but the task fails to document this deviation as a sanctioned exception or reference the Plan's justification. This is a silent weakening of the feature extraction requirement.

Tasks T004, T005, and T006 (creating schema contracts) are marked as unchecked (pending) in the task list, yet subsequent tasks (T015, T018, T020) explicitly depend on these artifacts for validation. If these schema tasks are not completed before the dependent tasks, the deliverables will lack the required validation contracts defined in the Plan's 'Contract Conformance' section, violating the data hygiene and validation constraints.

Task T026b defines the 'uniform baseline' as 'mean of the true coefficients distribution'. FR-005 and SC-001 define the uniform baseline as 'uniform weights from N(0,1)' (random baseline) and a separate 'uniform-weight baseline' (likely mean). The task conflates the random baseline definition with the uniform baseline, potentially weakening the statistical comparison required by FR-005.
